notes:
Next Destinies (BW4), released 2012-02-08. Sourced anchors: Flipside Gaming's rarity review gives the BW4 special-card rates — Pokemon-EX ~1:18 packs (~2/box), Full Art EX / Ultra Rare ~1:36 packs (~1/box), Secret Rare ~1:2 boxes (=1/72 packs). Bulbapedia confirms set structure (103 cards: 6 regular Pokemon-EX, 6 Full Art EX #94-99, 4 secret rares #100-103) which matches the given buckets EXACTLY (ex=6, ultra-rare=6, secret-rare=4, rare-holo=10). BW-era pack = 10 game cards (Basic Energy 11th card only began Sun & Moon): 5 common + 3 uncommon + 1 reverse-holo (common/uncommon 50/50) + 1 rare-or-better.

Rare-slot mapping (per-pack): ex=0.05556 (1/18), ultra-rare=0.02778 (1/36), secret-rare=0.01389 (1/72), rare-holo=0.23611 (derived: ~8.5 pure holos/box after the ~3.5 EX/FA/secret holo slots are removed from the ~12 holos/box era structure), rare=0.66666 (remainder). Sums to exactly 1.0.

Products: standard 36-pack Booster Display Box confirmed (DA Card World). NO ETB — Elite Trainer Boxes were introduced with Plasma Storm (Feb 2013), after Next Destinies, so etbPacks=null.

Confidence=low: the three special-card rates are published era/per-set figures but there is no large dedicated BW4 opening study, and the rare-holo vs non-holo split is reconstructed from era box structure rather than a counted sample. shipEv=true: a real booster product exists with sourceable rates.

Config written to /Users/alexandreott/Desktop/POKÉ EV/data/pull-rates/next-destinies.json and passes the project validator (npm run validate:data: "All datasets valid", 59 pull-rate sets). Vintage sealed-box premium will exceed EV/booster — expected for a 2012 set.
